nodes.label: What contributors should and should not change
nodes.norm_label: what contributors should and should not change

nodes.label: Add a new UI component safely
nodes.norm_label: add a new ui component safely

nodes.label: Offline-first caching strategy
nodes.rationale: Offline-first caching favors resilience over live updates; manual refresh is explicit rather than automatic, trading freshness for reliability when network is unavailable.

nodes.label: Tab + stack navigation model
nodes.rationale: The tab + stack model simplifies deep navigation at the cost of more route wiring (each tab gets its own stack navigator, shared detail routes duplicated across stacks).

nodes.label: Device-local preferences (no account sync)
nodes.rationale: User preferences and favorites are device-local only; there is no account sync, simplifying implementation at the cost of cross-device continuity.

nodes.label: Local notification capping/re-scheduling
nodes.rationale: Local notifications are fully re-scheduled and capped (180 max) to avoid hitting OS-imposed scheduled-notification limits.

nodes.label: Static JSON programme data assumption
nodes.rationale: Programme data is treated as static JSON; changes in upstream shape require corresponding updates in src/services/data.ts.

nodes.label: EXPO_PUBLIC_* base-URL resolution order
nodes.rationale: Web dev always uses EXPO_PUBLIC_WEB_PROXY_BASE (API_BASE ignored); other platforms use EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE if set, else production host; dev fetch failures fall back to production - keeps local CORS-proxy testing separate from native/prod resolution.

nodes.label: Cache key version-bump invalidation (conferenceCacheKey)
nodes.rationale: Bumping the version suffix in conferenceCacheKey (e.g. v2 to v3) when ConferenceData shape/normalization changes prevents stale cached payloads from being reused after a schema change.

nodes.label: Path alias convention (tsconfig/babel)
nodes.rationale: Imports use path aliases (@components/*, @screens/*, etc.) defined in tsconfig.json and babel.config.js; aliases must stay aligned when moving/renaming folders.

nodes.label: CI runs typecheck only, no test runner
nodes.rationale: No test runner is configured; CI enforces pnpm typecheck only, so manual verification of UI/navigation flows is the primary quality gate.
nodes.norm_label: ci runs typecheck only, no test runner

nodes.label: components/ folder boundary (presentational UI)
nodes.rationale: Components should stay presentational, receiving data/callbacks from screens; ScreenContainer is a documented intentional exception that reads settings/navigation state.

nodes.label: config/ folder boundary (static constants)
nodes.rationale: config/ centralizes app constants and conference metadata; must avoid side effects and runtime state, keeping values static and deterministic.

nodes.label: data/ folder boundary (declarative content)
nodes.rationale: data/ holds static bundled content only; no runtime logic or dynamic fetches allowed.

nodes.label: hooks/ folder boundary (shared side-effect logic)
nodes.rationale: hooks/ hosts logic spanning multiple screens/components, especially side-effectful flows; hooks should not render UI.

nodes.label: navigation/ folder boundary (routing only)
nodes.rationale: navigation/ centralizes route names, typed param lists, and stack/tab registration; must not reach into services or stores.

nodes.label: screens/ folder boundary (orchestration)
nodes.rationale: screens/ are top-level views orchestrating data loading/state/layout for a route; reusable UI belongs in components/, logic in hooks/ or utils/.

nodes.label: services/ folder boundary (data access only)
nodes.rationale: services/ handles data access and normalization (network requests, caching); must avoid UI, navigation, or direct component coupling, returning typed data only.

nodes.label: store/ folder boundary (global state + persistence)
nodes.rationale: store/ contains global state containers and persistence for settings, conference data, and favorites; data parsing belongs in services/, not store/.

nodes.label: types/ folder boundary (types only)
nodes.rationale: types/ holds TypeScript interfaces for raw API payloads and normalized app models; no runtime code.

nodes.label: utils/ folder boundary (pure helpers + isolated side effects)
nodes.rationale: utils/ holds pure helpers and isolated side-effect wrappers (formatting, time, notifications, calendar, storage); avoid React hooks and UI to keep logic testable.
nodes.norm_label: utils/ folder boundary (pure helpers + isolated side effects)
